title: Secret of Monkey Island - Main Theme (MT-32 voices per ROL chunk)
arranger: Claude (SMI SNES port; Munt MT-32 samples, ROL-source channels)
_about_assignments: ROL chunk targets the Roland MT-32: program numbers are MT-32 patches, translated via ScummVM _mt32ToGm. ch1=77 flute lead, ch2=103 xylophone, ch3=9 organ pad, ch4=104 marimba arp, ch5=65 acoustic bass, ch6=32 fantasia bells, ch7=110 blown bottle. ch6+ch7 are temporally disjoint and share spc7 via merge_ch. Samples rendered dry through Munt at 32kHz (native MT-32 = native SNES DSP rate).
_timer_note: TAD caps timer at 256 (tick <= 32ms) and zenlen at 255, so a 2000ms quarter cannot be zenlen/4. note_scale=2 writes quarters as halves: 96 ticks x 20.875ms (timer 167) = 2004ms = 29.94 BPM (+0.2%). All musical lengths integer (64th = 6 ticks).
_echo_note: Faking MT-32 reverb with the 32ms echo unit: a single-tap flutter reads as echo far more aggressively than diffuse reverb at equal energy, so run the level/feedback BELOW the energy-match point. EFB 116/EVOL 48 matched the ref tail dB-for-dB but Chad called it too much echo; EFB 108/EVOL 32 = ~46dB/s, ~0.6s audible bloom.
